name: AZQ Stage 2 Wave B Checks
purpose: Define task-specific automated checks for Stage 2 Wave B so the Codex runner can evaluate the first Formam command-layer work without hardcoding task logic into the runner.

baseline_checks.name: compileall
baseline_checks.description: Verify the azq package still compiles after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: unit_tests
baseline_checks.description: Verify the existing regression suite still passes after the patch.

baseline_checks.name: formam_storage_import
baseline_checks.description: Verify the Formam storage module still imports cleanly.

STAGE2-WAVEB-01.description: Implement azq form build as a thin command-layer writer.

checks.name: form_build_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the build module exposes a command-layer entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import build
assert any(hasattr(build, name) for name in ['build_form', 'build_deliverables_for_goal', 'form_build']), 'missing Formam build entrypoint'

checks.name: form_build_writes_canonical_deliverable_and_map
checks.description: Verify the build path can validate a parent goal, allocate an id, and write one deliverable plus one goal map through storage helpers.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import build
entry = getattr(build, 'build_form', None) or getattr(build, 'build_deliverables_for_goal', None) or getattr(build, 'form_build', None)
assert entry is not None, 'build entrypoint not found'
written_deliverables = []
written_maps = []
with patch('azq.formam.build.validate_parent_goal', return_value={'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Parent goal', 'status': 'active', 'created': '2026-03-17', 'description': 'desc', 'derived_from': []}), patch('azq.formam.build.next_deliverable_id', return_value='DELIV_001'), patch('azq.formam.build.write_deliverable', side_effect=lambda record: written_deliverables.append(dict(record))), patch('azq.formam.build.load_deliverables_for_goal', return_value=[]), patch('azq.formam.build.write_goal_map', side_effect=lambda record: written_maps.append(dict(record))):
    entry('FINIS_001')
assert written_deliverables, 'no deliverable was written'
assert written_maps, 'no goal map was written'
assert written_deliverables[0]['deliverable_id'] == 'DELIV_001'
assert written_deliverables[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
assert written_maps[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'

human_checks: The build behavior stays intentionally small and deterministic rather than drifting into Agenda-style planning.
The command module delegates persistence and file-format decisions to azq/formam/storage.py.

STAGE2-WAVEB-02.description: Implement azq form list as a thin presentation layer.

checks.name: form_list_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the deliverables module exposes a listing entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import deliverables
assert any(hasattr(deliverables, name) for name in ['list_deliverables', 'show_deliverables']), 'missing deliverable listing entrypoint'

checks.name: form_list_reads_canonical_deliverables
checks.description: Verify the list command prints canonical deliverables and preserves the no-deliverables case.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import deliverables
entry = getattr(deliverables, 'list_deliverables', None) or getattr(deliverables, 'show_deliverables', None)
assert entry is not None, 'listing entrypoint not found'
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.formam.deliverables.load_all_deliverables', return_value=[]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry()
assert 'No deliverables' in buf.getvalue()
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.formam.deliverables.load_all_deliverables', return_value=[{'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Visible artifact', 'artifact_description': 'desc', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-17'}]):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry()
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'DELIV_001' in out
assert 'FINIS_001' in out
assert 'Visible artifact' in out
assert 'drafted' in out

human_checks: deliverables.py stays presentation-only rather than becoming a second repository layer.
The terminal output is simple, readable, and centered on deliverables.

STAGE2-WAVEB-03.description: Implement azq form show with exact-id deliverable inspection.

checks.name: form_show_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the deliverables module exposes an exact-id inspection entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import deliverables
assert any(hasattr(deliverables, name) for name in ['show_deliverable', 'inspect_deliverable']), 'missing deliverable show entrypoint'

checks.name: form_show_loads_exact_deliverable_and_prints_fields
checks.description: Verify the show command loads one canonical deliverable by exact id and prints the key fields.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import deliverables
entry = getattr(deliverables, 'show_deliverable', None) or getattr(deliverables, 'inspect_deliverable', None)
assert entry is not None, 'show entrypoint not found'
record = {'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Inspect me', 'artifact_description': 'Visible artifact description', 'dependencies': ['DELIV_000'], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-17'}
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.formam.deliverables.load_deliverable', return_value=record):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry('DELIV_001')
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'DELIV_001' in out
assert 'FINIS_001' in out
assert 'Inspect me' in out
assert 'Visible artifact description' in out
assert 'DELIV_000' in out

checks.name: form_show_missing_id_fails_cleanly
checks.description: Verify the show command handles a missing exact deliverable id cleanly.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import deliverables
entry = getattr(deliverables, 'show_deliverable', None) or getattr(deliverables, 'inspect_deliverable', None)
assert entry is not None, 'show entrypoint not found'
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('azq.formam.deliverables.load_deliverable', return_value=None):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        entry('DELIV_999')
out = buf.getvalue()
assert 'not found' in out.lower()

human_checks: Exact-id lookup stays exact and does not drift into fuzzy search.
The inspection output stays artifact-focused rather than task-focused.

STAGE2-WAVEB-04.description: Implement azq form map as a thin command-layer map refresher.

checks.name: form_map_entrypoint_exists
checks.description: Verify the maps module exposes a map refresh entrypoint.
checks.code:
from azq.formam import maps
assert any(hasattr(maps, name) for name in ['build_goal_map', 'refresh_goal_map', 'form_map']), 'missing goal-map entrypoint'

checks.name: form_map_builds_from_goal_deliverables
checks.description: Verify the map command validates the parent goal, loads deliverables for that goal, and writes one canonical map.
checks.code:
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq.formam import maps
entry = getattr(maps, 'build_goal_map', None) or getattr(maps, 'refresh_goal_map', None) or getattr(maps, 'form_map', None)
assert entry is not None, 'map entrypoint not found'
written_maps = []
with patch('azq.formam.maps.validate_parent_goal', return_value={'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Parent goal', 'status': 'active', 'created': '2026-03-17', 'description': '', 'derived_from': []}), patch('azq.formam.maps.load_deliverables_for_goal', return_value=[{'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_001', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'One', 'artifact_description': 'desc', 'dependencies': [], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-17'}, {'deliverable_id': 'DELIV_002', 'goal_id': 'FINIS_001', 'title': 'Two', 'artifact_description': 'desc', 'dependencies': ['DELIV_001'], 'status': 'drafted', 'created': '2026-03-17'}]), patch('azq.formam.maps.write_goal_map', side_effect=lambda record: written_maps.append(dict(record))):
    entry('FINIS_001')
assert written_maps, 'no goal map was written'
assert written_maps[0]['goal_id'] == 'FINIS_001'
assert 'DELIV_001' in written_maps[0]['deliverable_ids']
assert 'DELIV_002' in written_maps[0]['deliverable_ids']

human_checks: maps.py remains a thin command-layer module over storage helpers.
The map output stays conservative and human-readable rather than inventing speculative planning structure.

STAGE2-WAVEB-05.description: Wire Formam commands into the CLI.

checks.name: cli_contains_form_branch_and_usage
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'azq form build <goal_id>',
    'azq form list',
    'azq form show <deliverable_id>',
    'azq form map <goal_id>',
    'elif cmd == "form":'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
PY
checks.description: Verify the CLI advertises and routes the first Formam command family.

checks.name: cli_form_branch_dispatches_to_modules
checks.command:
python - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
text = Path('azq/cli.py').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
for snippet in [
    'from azq.formam.build import',
    'from azq.formam.deliverables import',
    'from azq.formam.maps import'
]:
    assert snippet in text, snippet
assert 'ensure_finis_storage_ready()' in text
PY
checks.description: Verify the CLI wires Formam command dispatch through dedicated modules and preserves Finis migration readiness.

checks.name: cli_form_help_smoke
checks.description: Verify the CLI form branch prints usage cleanly when called without a Formam subcommand.
checks.code:
import io
from contextlib import redirect_stdout
from unittest.mock import patch
from azq import cli
buf = io.StringIO()
with patch('sys.argv', ['azq', 'form']):
    with redirect_stdout(buf):
        cli.main()
out = buf.getvalue().lower()
assert 'form' in out
assert 'build' in out
assert 'list' in out
assert 'show' in out
assert 'map' in out

human_checks: The CLI wiring stays narrow and does not smuggle in Agenda, doctor, or status behavior.
The first public Formam loop feels consistent with the existing terminal command style.
